texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: ANNEX B. BUSINESS ENTRY-ANNOTATED QUESTIONNAIRE
texts.text: ANNEX B. BUSINESS ENTRY-ANNOTATED QUESTIONNAIRE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Annex  B  consists  of  a  Glossary  and  Annotated  Questionnaire  for  Business  Entry.  The  Annotated Questionnaire provides the mapping between each indicator and the corresponding question(s).
texts.text: Annex  B  consists  of  a  Glossary  and  Annotated  Questionnaire  for  Business  Entry.  The  Annotated Questionnaire provides the mapping between each indicator and the corresponding question(s).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Glossary
texts.text: Glossary

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Beneficial owner: The natural person that ultimately owns or controls a company, even if the title to the property is under another name (i.e., the ownership or control is exercised through a chain of ownership or by means of control other than direct shareholding).
texts.text: Beneficial owner: The natural person that ultimately owns or controls a company, even if the title to the property is under another name (i.e., the ownership or control is exercised through a chain of ownership or by means of control other than direct shareholding).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Beneficial  ownership  information: Elements  that  would  allow  to  identify  a  beneficial  owner:  name, identification number, address or date of birth.
texts.text: Beneficial  ownership  information: Elements  that  would  allow  to  identify  a  beneficial  owner:  name, identification number, address or date of birth.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Centralized  registry: A  single,  unified  authority  or  database  that  serves  as  the  official  repository  for company registration and related information within an economy.
texts.text: Centralized  registry: A  single,  unified  authority  or  database  that  serves  as  the  official  repository  for company registration and related information within an economy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Electronic company registration process: A process during which the entrepreneur can fully register a company online without the need to go in person to the business registry, or any other authority, to present or obtain hard copies of documents.
texts.text: Electronic company registration process: A process during which the entrepreneur can fully register a company online without the need to go in person to the business registry, or any other authority, to present or obtain hard copies of documents.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Foreign company: A company in which foreign individuals or entities hold any ownership stake or exert any level of participation.
texts.text: Foreign company: A company in which foreign individuals or entities hold any ownership stake or exert any level of participation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Foreign entrepreneur: An entrepreneur from a foreign country residing in the measured economy.
texts.text: Foreign entrepreneur: An entrepreneur from a foreign country residing in the measured economy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Fully electronic registry/database: A platform where all data is stored and accessible electronically.
texts.text: Fully electronic registry/database: A platform where all data is stored and accessible electronically.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Nominee shareholder: A person or legal entity that is officially registered as the holder of shares in a company with limited liability, but who holds those shares on behalf of another person-the beneficial owner-under a  legal  or  contractual  arrangement.  The  nominee  does  not  typically  exercise  control  or receive economic benefits from the shares, which remain with the beneficial owner. Nominee arrangements are often used to provide anonymity or to fulfill legal or administrative requirements, and their recognition and regulations vary across legal systems. For example, an entrepreneur may ask their lawyer or a trusted associate to be listed as the legal shareholder of a company while retaining actual ownership and decisionmaking power behind the scenes.
texts.text: Nominee shareholder: A person or legal entity that is officially registered as the holder of shares in a company with limited liability, but who holds those shares on behalf of another person-the beneficial owner-under a  legal  or  contractual  arrangement.  The  nominee  does  not  typically  exercise  control  or receive economic benefits from the shares, which remain with the beneficial owner. Nominee arrangements are often used to provide anonymity or to fulfill legal or administrative requirements, and their recognition and regulations vary across legal systems. For example, an entrepreneur may ask their lawyer or a trusted associate to be listed as the legal shareholder of a company while retaining actual ownership and decisionmaking power behind the scenes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Operating licenses: Any form of permission granted by a public authority that enables the company to carry out a business activity within a jurisdiction.
texts.text: Operating licenses: Any form of permission granted by a public authority that enables the company to carry out a business activity within a jurisdiction.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Paid-in minimum capital: The minimum amount of capital that a company's founders or shareholders are legally required to contribute and deposit into the company, usually at the time of incorporation.
texts.text: Paid-in minimum capital: The minimum amount of capital that a company's founders or shareholders are legally required to contribute and deposit into the company, usually at the time of incorporation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Public  authority: Any  government  organization  and/or  public  agency  that  have  been  established  and authorized by the government to oversee and regulate certain aspects of commerce and industry within a jurisdiction They include, for example, business registry, ministry of justice, chamber of commerce, tax authority, social security, municipality, etc.
texts.text: Public  authority: Any  government  organization  and/or  public  agency  that  have  been  established  and authorized by the government to oversee and regulate certain aspects of commerce and industry within a jurisdiction They include, for example, business registry, ministry of justice, chamber of commerce, tax authority, social security, municipality, etc.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Publicly  funded  programs: Initiatives  financed  by  the  government,  such  as  grant  schemes,  training programs, or business advisory services. These exclude privately funded or donor-run programs.
texts.text: Publicly  funded  programs: Initiatives  financed  by  the  government,  such  as  grant  schemes,  training programs, or business advisory services. These exclude privately funded or donor-run programs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Publicly available information: Information that is accessible to the public without restriction, regardless of whether a fee is required to access it.
texts.text: Publicly available information: Information that is accessible to the public without restriction, regardless of whether a fee is required to access it.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Risk-based approach: The requirements, procedures, and scrutiny applied to businesses when obtaining operating licenses or permits that are proportionate to the level of risk their activities pose.
texts.text: Risk-based approach: The requirements, procedures, and scrutiny applied to businesses when obtaining operating licenses or permits that are proportionate to the level of risk their activities pose.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Service standards: Officially published information outlining the expected steps, processing times, and costs associated with registering a [LLC legal form] in [economy]. They typically define what applicants can expect from the responsible authority in terms of procedure, efficiency, and transparency during the company registration process.
texts.text: Service standards: Officially published information outlining the expected steps, processing times, and costs associated with registering a [LLC legal form] in [economy]. They typically define what applicants can expect from the responsible authority in terms of procedure, efficiency, and transparency during the company registration process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Simple standard registration form: A standard template for business incorporation that can be used by entrepreneurs to register the company by themselves without the help of third-party intermediaries such as lawyers or notaries.
texts.text: Simple standard registration form: A standard template for business incorporation that can be used by entrepreneurs to register the company by themselves without the help of third-party intermediaries such as lawyers or notaries.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sociodemographic  groups: Groups  categorized  by  ethnicity/race,  gender,  migration  status,  religious affiliation, etc.
texts.text: Sociodemographic  groups: Groups  categorized  by  ethnicity/race,  gender,  migration  status,  religious affiliation, etc.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Statutory information: The  legally required details about a company that must be registered with the business registry or relevant authority. This typically includes the company's name, legal form, registered address, directors and shareholders, share capital, and any other information mandated by law.
texts.text: Statutory information: The  legally required details about a company that must be registered with the business registry or relevant authority. This typically includes the company's name, legal form, registered address, directors and shareholders, share capital, and any other information mandated by law.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Third-party intermediaries: Professionals-such  as  notaries,  lawyers,  accountants,  or  similar  service providers-who are authorized to incorporate a company on behalf of an entrepreneur.
texts.text: Third-party intermediaries: Professionals-such  as  notaries,  lawyers,  accountants,  or  similar  service providers-who are authorized to incorporate a company on behalf of an entrepreneur.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: BUSINESS ENTRY QUESTIONNAIRE
texts.text: BUSINESS ENTRY QUESTIONNAIRE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The tables below present all indicators (including their components, if applicable) under each pillar, with a reference to the corresponding question number in parenthesis. The questions are listed before each table for ease of reference.
texts.text: The tables below present all indicators (including their components, if applicable) under each pillar, with a reference to the corresponding question number in parenthesis. The questions are listed before each table for ease of reference.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For Y/N questions, the Y response accounts for the score and is considered as the good practice, unless otherwise indicated with the sign 'Y/N; N - good practice'.
texts.text: For Y/N questions, the Y response accounts for the score and is considered as the good practice, unless otherwise indicated with the sign 'Y/N; N - good practice'.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the tables that follow, 'AND' means all referenced questions must have a good practice response to obtain a score on the indicator.
texts.text: In the tables that follow, 'AND' means all referenced questions must have a good practice response to obtain a score on the indicator.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the tables that follow, 'OR' means one or more referenced questions must have a good practice response to obtain a score on the indicator.
texts.text: In the tables that follow, 'OR' means one or more referenced questions must have a good practice response to obtain a score on the indicator.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Certain questions are marked as "not scored,' which indicates that they do not affect the score in any way. The purpose of these questions is to further inform and refine the questions design for subsequent years of the  rollout  phase,  as  needed,  as  well  as  to  substantiate  and  provide  further  information  for  the  scored questions.
texts.text: Certain questions are marked as "not scored,' which indicates that they do not affect the score in any way. The purpose of these questions is to further inform and refine the questions design for subsequent years of the  rollout  phase,  as  needed,  as  well  as  to  substantiate  and  provide  further  information  for  the  scored questions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Some questions serve as a filter for one or more questions. In these cases, notes such as 'Y → provide response to question …' or 'N → provide response to question …' indicate whether the filtered questions should  be  asked  based  on  the  response  to  the  filter  question.  This  does  not  automatically  imply  that other subsequent questions are skipped, unless explicitly stated.
texts.text: Some questions serve as a filter for one or more questions. In these cases, notes such as 'Y → provide response to question …' or 'N → provide response to question …' indicate whether the filtered questions should  be  asked  based  on  the  response  to  the  filter  question.  This  does  not  automatically  imply  that other subsequent questions are skipped, unless explicitly stated.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.1 INFORMATION AND PROCEDURAL STANDARDS
texts.text: 1.1 INFORMATION AND PROCEDURAL STANDARDS

texts.orig: 1.1.1 Company Information Filing Requirements
texts.text: 1.1.1 Company Information Filing Requirements

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Does the legal framework require entrepreneurs to obtain mandatory company name approval from the business registry or another corresponding public authority before registering a new company? (Y/N)
texts.text: Does the legal framework require entrepreneurs to obtain mandatory company name approval from the business registry or another corresponding public authority before registering a new company? (Y/N)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Y → provide response to questions 2, 3 and 4.
texts.text: Y → provide response to questions 2, 3 and 4.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2. Does the legal framework establish specific rules for rejecting proposed company names by the business registry or another corresponding public authority? (Y/N)

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Does  the  legal  framework  require  the  entrepreneur's  identity  to  be  verified  by  the  business registry, a public authority, or an authorized intermediary (e.g. a notary or lawyer) to register a company? (Y/N)

(Y/N)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.1.4 Risk-Based Assessment for Operating Business Licenses
texts.text: 1.1.4 Risk-Based Assessment for Operating Business Licenses

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 33. Does the legal framework require a risk-based approach to business licensing for new companies, where licensing rules depend on the company's activity and level of risk (e.g., low-risk activities may have simpler or no licensing, high-risk activities require stricter licensing)? (Y/N) Y → provide response to question 34.
texts.text: Does the legal framework require a risk-based approach to business licensing for new companies, where licensing rules depend on the company's activity and level of risk (e.g., low-risk activities may have simpler or no licensing, high-risk activities require stricter licensing)? (Y/N) Y → provide response to question 34.

texts.text: Does the law/regulation provide the option of indefinite operating licenses for low-risk activities? (Y/N)

texts.orig: 1.2.1 Domestic Companies
texts.text: 1.2.1 Domestic Companies

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 35. Does the legal framework require a mandated paid-in minimum capital for companies owned by domestic entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework require a mandated paid-in minimum capital for companies owned by domestic entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 36. Does the legal framework require a verification of an entrepreneur's criminal history to register a company and begin general business operations? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework require a verification of an entrepreneur's criminal history to register a company and begin general business operations? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 37. Does the legal framework require obtaining a general business operating license to register a company and begin general business operations? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework require obtaining a general business operating license to register a company and begin general business operations? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 38. Does the legal framework mandate any additional or different requirements for entrepreneurs based on sociodemographic characteristics to register a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework mandate any additional or different requirements for entrepreneurs based on sociodemographic characteristics to register a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 39. Does the legal framework mandate any additional or different requirements for entrepreneurs based on sociodemographic characteristics (e.g., gender, ethnicity, migration status) to open a business bank account for a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework mandate any additional or different requirements for entrepreneurs based on sociodemographic characteristics (e.g., gender, ethnicity, migration status) to open a business bank account for a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: The following questions pertain to nationals only, not foreign investors.
texts.text: The following questions pertain to nationals only, not foreign investors.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 40. To what extent does the legal framework impose restrictions on 100% ownership by private companies  or  individuals  across  business  sectors?  (i.e.,  is  private  participation  capped,  and public/state participation required in some areas?)
texts.text: To what extent does the legal framework impose restrictions on 100% ownership by private companies  or  individuals  across  business  sectors?  (i.e.,  is  private  participation  capped,  and public/state participation required in some areas?)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note: Please select the option that best describes the legal framework.
texts.text: Note: Please select the option that best describes the legal framework.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 40a. No restrictions: Law allows 100% private ownership in all sectors; no limits on domestic private participation.
texts.text: 40a. No restrictions: Law allows 100% private ownership in all sectors; no limits on domestic private participation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 40b. Security-only restrictions: Limits apply solely to defense or national security sectors requiring state participation or control.
texts.text: 40b. Security-only restrictions: Limits apply solely to defense or national security sectors requiring state participation or control.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 40c. Selected sectors restricted: Full private ownership barred in a few strategic or public-interest sectors (e.g., energy, mining, banking, telecom, media).
texts.text: 40c. Selected sectors restricted: Full private ownership barred in a few strategic or public-interest sectors (e.g., energy, mining, banking, telecom, media).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 40d. Widespread restrictions: The law limits private ownership across multiple sectors, requiring public participation in many commercial and industrial activities, beyond those listed in option 3.
texts.text: 40d. Widespread restrictions: The law limits private ownership across multiple sectors, requiring public participation in many commercial and industrial activities, beyond those listed in option 3.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.2.2 Foreign Companies
texts.text: 1.2.2 Foreign Companies

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 41. Does  the  legal  framework  require  a  mandated  paid-in  minimum  capital  requirement  for companies owned by foreign entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does  the  legal  framework  require  a  mandated  paid-in  minimum  capital  requirement  for companies owned by foreign entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 42. Does the legal framework set a maximum percentage of shares that foreign investors (natural or legal persons) may own in a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework set a maximum percentage of shares that foreign investors (natural or legal persons) may own in a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 43. Does the legal framework require foreign entrepreneurs to undergo a foreign direct investment (FDI)  screening  or  obtain  prior  approval  from  a  government  authority  before  registering  a company or commencing operations? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework require foreign entrepreneurs to undergo a foreign direct investment (FDI)  screening  or  obtain  prior  approval  from  a  government  authority  before  registering  a company or commencing operations? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 44. Does the legal framework establish a minimum investment requirement that applies specifically or differently to foreign entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework establish a minimum investment requirement that applies specifically or differently to foreign entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 45. Does the legal framework impose any nationality (citizenship) on the directors of a foreign owned company? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework impose any nationality (citizenship) on the directors of a foreign owned company? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 46. Does the legislation have any requirement with respect to the nationality of the board members for companies owned by foreign entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legislation have any requirement with respect to the nationality of the board members for companies owned by foreign entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 47. Does  the  legal  framework  in  any  way  mandate  a  minimum  number,  percentage,  or  ratio  of national employees in companies? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does  the  legal  framework  in  any  way  mandate  a  minimum  number,  percentage,  or  ratio  of national employees in companies? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 48. Does the legal framework require foreign entrepreneurs to have a local partner when establishing a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does the legal framework require foreign entrepreneurs to have a local partner when establishing a company? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 49. Does  the  legal  framework  impose  any  restrictions  on  dividend  distribution  for  foreign entrepreneurs  (such  as  lower  payment  caps,  additional  paperwork,  special  approvals,  or processing  delays)  that  do  not  apply  to  domestic  entrepreneurs,  aside  from  standard  tax obligations? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Does  the  legal  framework  impose  any  restrictions  on  dividend  distribution  for  foreign entrepreneurs  (such  as  lower  payment  caps,  additional  paperwork,  special  approvals,  or processing  delays)  that  do  not  apply  to  domestic  entrepreneurs,  aside  from  standard  tax obligations? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 50. Are  there  any  legal  restrictions  on  foreign  entrepreneurs  opening  or  maintaining  foreign currency bank accounts that do not apply to domestic entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)
texts.text: Are  there  any  legal  restrictions  on  foreign  entrepreneurs  opening  or  maintaining  foreign currency bank accounts that do not apply to domestic entrepreneurs? (Y/N; N - good practice)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 51. To what extent does the legal framework impose restrictions on full ownership by private foreign companies or individuals across business sectors?
texts.text: 51. To what extent does the legal framework impose restrictions on full ownership by private foreign companies or individuals across business sectors?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note: Please select the option that best describes the legal framework.
texts.text: Note: Please select the option that best describes the legal framework.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 51a.  No  restrictions:  The  legal  framework  imposes  no  limits  on  foreign  private  ownership;  100% foreign ownership is allowed in all sectors.
texts.text: 51a.  No  restrictions:  The  legal  framework  imposes  no  limits  on  foreign  private  ownership;  100% foreign ownership is allowed in all sectors.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 51b. Security-only restrictions: Limits apply solely to sectors tied to national security or defense (e.g., arms, military tech, defense contracting).
texts.text: 51b. Security-only restrictions: Limits apply solely to sectors tied to national security or defense (e.g., arms, military tech, defense contracting).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 51c. Selected sector restrictions: Foreign ownership is limited in a few strategic sectors (e.g., energy, mining, banking, telecom, media).
texts.text: 51c. Selected sector restrictions: Foreign ownership is limited in a few strategic sectors (e.g., energy, mining, banking, telecom, media).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 51d. Widespread restrictions (e.g. existence of a "negative list"): The law broadly restricts or prohibits foreign ownership across many sectors (e.g., transport, retail, construction, professional services).
texts.text: 51d. Widespread restrictions (e.g. existence of a "negative list"): The law broadly restricts or prohibits foreign ownership across many sectors (e.g., transport, retail, construction, professional services).

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Note simultaneity: consider in your calculations steps that can be completed together. Simultaneity of procedures refers to the ability for multiple regulatory or administrative steps to be carried out at the same time rather than sequentially; in such cases, time should be counted based on the longest overlapping procedure
texts.text: Note simultaneity: consider in your calculations steps that can be completed together. table_cells.text: PILLAR III-OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY OF BUSINESS ENTRY
The largest (most populous) city in the economy. The parameter applies to all questions under Pillar III.
The most common type of limited liability company (LLC) in the economy. The parameter applies to all questions under Pillar III.

Start-up capital is 5 times GNI per capita (for high income economies, start- up capital is 5 times USD 13,936), 5 shareholders and 5 employees for domestic companies. Start-up capital is USD$500,000, 5 shareholders, 12 employees for foreign companies. The parameter applies to all questions under Pillar III.

grid.text: PILLAR III-OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY OF BUSINESS ENTRY
PILLAR III-OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY OF BUSINESS ENTRY
The largest (most populous) city in the economy. The parameter applies to all questions under Pillar III.
The most common type of limited liability company (LLC) in the economy. The parameter applies to all questions under Pillar III.

Start-up capital is 5 times GNI per capita (for high income economies, start- up capital is 5 times USD 13,936), 5 shareholders and 5 employees for domestic companies. Start-up capital is USD$500,000, 5 shareholders, 12 employees for foreign companies. The parameter applies to all questions under Pillar III.
